summarization.a: X-ray diffraction patterns showing crystalline phases of the iron oxide film grown from ferrocene at 500 °C.
summarization.b: X-ray diffraction patterns showing crystalline phases of the iron oxide film grown from ferrocene at 400 °C.
summarization.c: X-ray diffraction patterns showing crystalline phases of the iron oxide film grown from (dimethylamino)ferrocene at 375 °C.

data_extraction.b:
| Species | 2θ, degree |
|---|---|
| Hematite FGA | Peak at 33.2° |
| Magnetite FGA | Peaks at 30.1°, 35.4°, 43.1°|
| Hematite as-deposited | Peaks at 30.1°, 33.2°, 35.4°, 43.1° |
data_extraction.c:
| Species | 2θ, degree |
|---|---|
| Magnetite FGA | Peaks at 30°, 35°, 43°, 54°, 57°, 64° |
| Magnetite as-deposited | Peaks at 30°, 35°, 43°, 57°, 64° |

c.question: How many ALD cycles were performed for the bottom panel?

a.question: How were the iron oxide films grown of which the results are shown here?
a.answer: The ALD cycle consisted of: 1 s iron-precursor pulse, 0.5 s purge, 1 s ozone pulse, 0.5 s purge. For the top and middle panel, ferrocene was used. For the bottom panel, (dimethylamino)ferrocene was used.

a.question: How did the process conditions differ between panels?
a.answer: The conditions differed in deposition temperature when the films were grown. These were 500 °C, 400 °C and 375 °C for the top, middle and bottom panel, respectively.

a.question: Which process conditions led exclusively to the growth of hematite?
a.answer: The top panel in which ferrocene was grown at 500 °C. Annealing these films did not lead to the formation of magnetite.
